posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Dear Experts,
I am trying to compute Toy based CLs limits using the latest recipe of the combine tool as given here [1]. I submitted jobs on GRID using the recipe mentioned in [2] following steps [3] and collected the result using hadd to get the final grid root file. Now, when I am trying to compute the limit using this grid file I am getting this message:
$combine datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --readHybridResults --grid=outputFile.root --quantileExpected 0.5
<<< Combine >>>
Invalid options: unrecognised option ‘–quantileExpected’
Invalid options: unrecognised option ‘–quantileExpected’
Use combine --help to get a list of all the allowed options
This is surprising since the recipe mentioned in [4] uses this very option to extract the limit from the grid file.
Am I missing something ?
Thanks.
[1]
http://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/
[2]
https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/runningthetool/#running-combine-jobs-on-the-grid
[3]

STEP TO MAKE THE ROO-WORKSPACE (datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root) FROM THE DATACARD

text2workspace.py datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.txt

STEP TO LAUNCH MED. EXP LIMIT COMPUTATION ON GRID USING THE ROO-WORKSPACE

combineTool.py -d datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --expectedFromGrid=0.5 -n _3l_1tau_ToyBasedCls_MedExp --singlePoint 0.0:5.0:0.1 -T 2000 -s -1 --saveToys --saveHybridResult -m 125 --job-mode crab3 --task-name grid-test_3l_1tau_ToyBasedCls_MedExp --custom-crab custom_crab.py
[4]
http://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/commonstatsmethods/#computing-the-observed-bayesian-limit-for-arbitrary-models

Dear Experts,
I am trying to compute Toy based CLs limits using the latest recipe of the combine tool as given here [1]. I submitted jobs on GRID using the recipe mentioned in [2] following steps [3] and collected the result using hadd to get the final grid root file. Now, when I am trying to compute the limit using this grid file I am getting this message:

$combine datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --readHybridResults --grid=outputFile.root --quantileExpected 0.5

<<< Combine >>>
Invalid options: unrecognised option ‘–quantileExpected’
Invalid options: unrecognised option ‘–quantileExpected’
Use combine --help to get a list of all the allowed options

This is surprising since the recipe mentioned in [4] uses this very option to extract the limit from the grid file.

Am I missing something ?

Thanks.

[1]

http://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/

[2]

https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/runningthetool/#running-combine-jobs-on-the-grid

[3]

  1. STEP TO MAKE THE ROO-WORKSPACE (datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root) FROM THE DATACARD

text2workspace.py datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.txt

  1. STEP TO LAUNCH MED. EXP LIMIT COMPUTATION ON GRID USING THE ROO-WORKSPACE

combineTool.py -d datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --expectedFromGrid=0.5 -n _3l_1tau_ToyBasedCls_MedExp --singlePoint 0.0:5.0:0.1 -T 2000 -s -1 --saveToys --saveHybridResult -m 125 --job-mode crab3 --task-name grid-test_3l_1tau_ToyBasedCls_MedExp --custom-crab custom_crab.py

[4]

http://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/commonstatsmethods/#computing-the-observed-bayesian-limit-for-arbitrary-models

posts.text:
Hi ,
Did anyone figure out what is the issue here ?

Dear Experts,
I am trying to compute Toy based CLs limits using the latest recipe of the combine tool as given here [1]. I submitted jobs on GRID using the recipe mentioned in [2] following steps [3] and collected the result using hadd to get the final grid root file. Now, when I am trying to compute the limit using this grid file I am getting this message:

$combine datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --readHybridResults --grid=outputFile.root --quantileExpected 0.5

Use combine --help to get a list of all the allowed options

This is surprising since the recipe mentioned in [4] uses this very option to extract the limit from the grid file.

Am I missing something ?

[1]

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Ram,
I think this was a small mistake in the example in the docs (now fixed) - it should be --expectedFromGrid=0.5, like in your example [3].
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Ram,

I think this was a small mistake in the example in the docs (now fixed) - it should be --expectedFromGrid=0.5, like in your example [3].

cheers,
Andrew

posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
Now I get this:
ramkrishna~$combine datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --readHybridResults --grid=outputFile.root --expectedFromGrid=0.5
<<< Combine >>>

random number generator seed is 123456
using the Profile Likelihood test statistics modified for upper limits (Q_LHC)
method used is HybridNew
Caught exception The grid must contain at least 2 points.
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Andrew,
Now I get this:
ramkrishna~$combine datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --readHybridResults --grid=outputFile.root --expectedFromGrid=0.5

<<< Combine >>>

random number generator seed is 123456
using the Profile Likelihood test statistics modified for upper limits (Q_LHC)
method used is HybridNew
Caught exception The grid must contain at least 2 points.

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Ram,
Assuming there actually are more than two points in the file, try adding -m 125. This needs to be the same value as when you saved the test stat distributions.
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Ram,

Assuming there actually are more than two points in the file, try adding -m 125. This needs to be the same value as when you saved the test stat distributions.

cheers,
Andrew

posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
Thanks. This worked. I have a follow up question though. I had submitted Toy based CLs limit CRAB jobs for a grid of scan points with 2000 toys per scan-point (with random seed) in the first round. Now if I want to throw more toys to get more statistics (say for the -2 sigma band only), what should I do ? Launch a new round with the same settings of combine and more toys and later hadd the output root files from the previous and the current round of CRAB jobs or something more (to make sure I don’t overwrite anything from the previous round of toys) ?
Thanks.
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Andrew,
Thanks. This worked. I have a follow up question though. I had submitted Toy based CLs limit CRAB jobs for a grid of scan points with 2000 toys per scan-point (with random seed) in the first round. Now if I want to throw more toys to get more statistics (say for the -2 sigma band only), what should I do ? Launch a new round with the same settings of combine and more toys and later hadd the output root files from the previous and the current round of CRAB jobs or something more (to make sure I don’t overwrite anything from the previous round of toys) ?

Thanks.

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Ram,
Yes, that’s the way to do it - just run more jobs on the set of signal strength points you want to increase the statistics for, using different random number seeds each time, then hadd all the results together. Combine will merge test statistic distributions coming from the same r value.
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Ram,

Yes, that’s the way to do it - just run more jobs on the set of signal strength points you want to increase the statistics for, using different random number seeds each time, then hadd all the results together. Combine will merge test statistic distributions coming from the same r value.

cheers,
Andrew

posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
I see that the jobs with more toys are dying at an alarming rate on CRAB [*]. The dominant exit code is 50660 (Application terminated by wrapper because using too much RAM (RSS)). Currently I have set no requirement on the amount of RAM used per job. What should I do to ensure job success ?
[*]
Jobs status:                    failed       		 94.1% (48/51)
running      		  2.0% ( 1/51)
transferring 		  3.9% ( 2/51)
No publication information (publication has been disabled in the CRAB configuration file)
Error Summary: (use crab status --verboseErrors for details about the errors)
44 jobs failed with exit code 50660
4 jobs failed with exit code 50664
Have a look at https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/viewauth/CMSPublic/JobExitCodes for a description of the exit codes.

Runtime: 0:00:00 min, 20:50:32 max, 14:24:07 ave
CPU eff: 96% min, 134% max, 100% ave
Waste: 376:14:58 (34% of total)

posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Hi,
I resubmitted CRAB jobs with the line: “config.JobType.numCores = 4” in the “custom_crab.py” file and the line: “process.options = cms.untracked.PSet(numberOfThreads = cms.untracked.uint32(4))” inside the $CMSSW_BASE/CombineHarvester/CombineTools/scripts/do_nothing_cfg.py" file (which is used by combineTool.py file while creating the crab submission PSet in the crabConfig.py file [*]). I see that the jobs still fail with a failure rate of 50-90% and the majority of failed jobs have CRAB Exit code of 50660 (using too much RAM). FYI, my CRAB submission command is also given here [**].

config.Site.storageSite = ‘T2_EE_Estonia’

[**]

combineTool.py -d datacard_3l_1tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --expectedFromGrid=0.025 -n _3l_1tau_ToyBasedCls_2SigmaMinusNew2 --singlePoint 0.0:2.0:0.04 -T 10000 -s -1 --saveToys --saveHybridResult -m 125 --job-mode crab3 --task-name grid-test_3l_1tau_ToyBasedCls_2SigmaMinusNew2 --custom-crab custom_crab.py

Any idea as to what went wrong now ?

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Ram,
I think you changed the wrong option - IIRC crab is not like condor at CERN - the nCPU and memory are not tied together, so you need to set the value of maxMemoryMB in the config, not numCores. I would also consider running fewer toys in a single job, since there will be some correlation with the memory required.
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Ram,

I think you changed the wrong option - IIRC crab is not like condor at CERN - the nCPU and memory are not tied together, so you need to set the value of maxMemoryMB in the config, not numCores. I would also consider running fewer toys in a single job, since there will be some correlation with the memory required.

cheers,
Andrew

posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
The CRAB Twiki clearly says that:
maxMemoryMB integer Maximum amount of memory (in MB) a job is allowed to use. Defaults to 2000.
|numCores|integer|Number of requested cores per job. Defaults to 1. If you increase this value to run multi-threaded cmsRun, you may need to increase maxMemoryMB as well.

In the CMSSW parameter-set configuration you may require also the number of streams to be larger than one per thread, which affects the memory consumption too.

My colleagues here at KBFI told me that you can’t increase maxMemoryMB to more than 2000 or 2500 in a job since that is the maximum memory available per core in CRAB. So, if you want to increase your memory then the only way left is to increase numCores in the crab_Config.py (and numberofThreads per job in the CRAB PSet accordingly).
Correct me if I am wrong.
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Andrew,
The CRAB Twiki clearly says that:

maxMemoryMB integer Maximum amount of memory (in MB) a job is allowed to use. Defaults to 2000.

|numCores|integer|Number of requested cores per job. Defaults to 1. If you increase this value to run multi-threaded cmsRun, you may need to increase maxMemoryMB as well.

In the CMSSW parameter-set configuration you may require also the number of streams to be larger than one per thread, which affects the memory consumption too.

My colleagues here at KBFI told me that you can’t increase maxMemoryMB to more than 2000 or 2500 in a job since that is the maximum memory available per core in CRAB. So, if you want to increase your memory then the only way left is to increase numCores in the crab_Config.py (and numberofThreads per job in the CRAB PSet accordingly).

Correct me if I am wrong.

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
I don’t know, I thought the limit per core was higher now. But even if not, I think you will still need to explicitly request more memory, it doesn’t automatically increase it based on numCores. In any case, this is not really a combine issue so if you are still having problems you should ask the crab experts on the computing tools channel.
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:

In any case, this is not really a combine issue so if you are still having problems you should ask the crab experts on the computing tools channel.

cheers,
Andrew

posts.name: Ram Krishna Dewanjee
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
I was able to launch 100 CRAB job batches with each CRAB job batch running 100 toys for each of the 51 scan points=> 0.0:2.0:0.04 (so as to make a total of 10k toys in all for every scan point in the end) like this:
combineTool.py -d datacard_1l_3tau_Run2_SM.root -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-limits --expectedFromGrid=0.5 -n 1l_3tau_ToyBasedCls_MedExp{$BATCH_ID} --singlePoint 0.0:2.0:0.04 -T 100 -s -1 --saveToys --saveHybridResult -m 125 --job-mode crab3 --task-name grid-test_1l_3tau_ToyBasedCls_MedExp_{$BATCH_ID} --custom-crab custom_crab.py --saveGrid
where $BATCH_ID is the CRAB batch index running from 0 to 99. I have now untar-red and hadd-ed all the output root files on my side. FYI, here is how the final output root file looks like:

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Ram,
I think what you want is described in the documentation:
https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part3/commonstatsmethods/#complex-models
In particular, look for the instructions to use the plotTestStatCLs.py script.
cheers,
Andrew
